mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, driving upward revisions to corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive following months with month-over-month PAYEMS growth in more than 50% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, restrictive monetary policy materially elevates discount rates and depresses present values of future corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of walk-forward instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising unemployment and falling industrial production signal a contractionary phase where labor market deterioration amplifies supply-side weakness, compounding negative equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of rising UNRATE and falling INDPRO fails to produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of concurrent walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index readings above 180 force sustained restrictive monetary policy, raising real discount rates and depressing valuation multiples, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 180, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales signal robust consumer spending that translates into near-term revenue surprises for listed corporations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the coming week.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 52% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing output and inventory liquidation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as operating leverage reverses and profit margins compress.
failure_condition: Following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of weak housing starts and elevated unemployment signals simultaneous stress in interest-sensitive spending and labor income, producing multiplicative negative effects on corporate earnings expectations and equity returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5.0% and HOUST is below 2200 simultaneously, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with falling payroll employment indicate a restrictive monetary backdrop with contracting labor income, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as liquidity conditions tighten and recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases over prior 3 months and PAYEMS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS has risen over the prior 3 months and PAYEMS declines month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of walk-forward cases.
